title: Generic multi-runtime warm pool — warms the primary AND every active secondary over the RuntimeAdapter contract, with a global max-total cap

tests.summary: 3 passed — at start, warms a pool for the primary AND every active secondary (claude-code role=primary size 2; codex + gemini role=secondary size 1 each; warmedRuntimes lists all three) (multi-runtime-pool-ok); checkout draws from the named runtime's pool + drives its adapter, unknown runtime throws; the global max-total cap (8 default) is respected across runtimes (a secondary is trimmed to the remaining budget). Full suite 596 passed / 9 skipped / 0 failed.

e2e.summary: N/A — generic pool over the adapter; committed unit test with stub adapters (no live runtime) is the gate.

notes: MultiRuntimePool (packages/claude-pty/src/multi-runtime-pool.mjs) is built ONCE on the RuntimeAdapter contract + the existing WarmPtySessionPool: given a set of runtimes (each an adapter + warm size + role), it warms one WarmPtySessionPool per runtime via adapter.spawn — runtime-agnostic, so adding a runtime is just passing its adapter. At operative start it warms the primary AND every active secondary (Codex, Gemini), honoring a global max-total cap (default 8, matching the router-view pool-toggle default). status() exposes per-runtime warm/checked-out for the router view's Sessions section. The pool primitive itself (rotate, idle-cost, classify-through-pool) was already proven in MR0d; this slice makes it multi-runtime. Satisfies brief §3 token multi-runtime-pool-ok.
